The '''Dawferim Selfhood States''',<ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|I|174|Dawferim Selfhood States}}</ref> also know as the '''Dawferm Selfhood States''', were a group composed of several [[Star system/Legends|star systems]].<ref name="DESB" /> They included the [[Daupherm system/Legends|Daupherm system]],<ref name="Email" /> which was situated in the [[Southern Core]] portion of the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]].<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> The Dawferim Selfhood States shared their name with the [[Dawferim]], a [[Human/Legends|Human]] group that controlled more than thirty systems in the [[Daupherm Planet States]] region of the Core Worlds.<ref name="TEA" />

In the six [[Standard year/Legends|years]] following the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]],<ref name="DESB" /> between [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]] and [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]],<ref name="TEA" /> the systems of the Dawferim Selfhood States grouped together and formed a protective federation due to the lack of a centralized [[Galaxy/Legends|galactic]] government and the ongoing [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|conflict]] between the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Empire]] and the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]].<ref name="DESB" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|What the hell are the Botor Enclave and the Dawferm Selfhood States?|Daniel Wallace|<ref name="Blog">{{Blog|danwallace|81|Underappreciated Star Wars Writers: Michael Allen Horne}}</ref>}}
The Dawferm Selfhood States were first mentioned in the ''[[Dark Empire Sourcebook]]'', [[Michael Allen Horne]]'s [[1993]] sourcebook published by [[West End Games]] for use with ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="DESB" /> They were later identified as the Dawferim Selfhood States in ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'', a [[2008]] reference book written by [[Stephen J. Sansweet]] and [[Pablo Hidalgo]].<ref name="CSWE" />

In [[August]] [[2007]], [[Daniel Wallace]] stated in his blog that he and [[Jason Fry]] planned to make a reference to the Dawferm Selfhood States and the [[Botor Enclave/Legends|Botor Enclave]] in ''[[The Essential Atlas]]''.<ref name="Blog" /> However, the [[2009]] reference book ultimately did not mention the Dawferm Selfhood States.<ref name="TEA" />

In [[April]] [[2023]], Fry confirmed that the Daupherm system was the the origin point of both the Daupherm Planet States and the Dawferim Selfhood States.<ref name="Email" />
